debug_info.goal:
a b : ℝ
ha : 0 < a
hb : 0 < b
⊢ 0 ∉ uIcc a b

failed_attempts:
{ cases' h with h₁ h₂ <;>
      (try { simp_all [uIcc, le_of_lt] <;> linarith }) <;>
      (try { linarith }) })
  <;>
  simp_all [uIcc, le_of_lt] <;>
  linarith

    have h₃ : b ≠ 0 := by linarith
    -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus to compute the integral
    have h₄ : ∫ x in a..b, 1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 = (log b + 1 / b) - (log a + 1 / a) := by
      -- Compute the antiderivative and evaluate at the bounds
      have h₅ : ∫ x in a..b, 1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 = ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) := by rfl
      rw [h₅]
      -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus
      have h₆ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = (log b + 1 / b) - (log a + 1 / a) := by
        -- Compute the antiderivative and evaluate at the bounds
        have h₇ : a ≠ 0 := by linarith
        have h₈ : b ≠ 0 := by linarith
        have h₉ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = (log b + 1 / b) - (log a + 1 / a) := by
          -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus
          have h₁₀ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) + ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) := by
            -- Split the integral into two parts
            apply intervalIntegral.integral_add
            · -- Prove that 1/x is continuous on [a, b]
              apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
              exact Continuous.div continuous_const continuous_id fun x hx => by
                have h₁₁ : a ≤ x := by exact hx.1
                have h₁₂ : x ≤ b := by exact hx.2
                have h₁₃ : 0 < x := by linarith
                linarith
            · -- Prove that 1/x² is continuous on [a, b]
              apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
              have h₁₁ : Continuous fun x : ℝ => (1 : ℝ) / x ^ 2 := by

                have h₁₃ : Continuous fun x : ℝ => (1 : ℝ) / x ^ 2 := by
                  apply Continuous.div
                  · exact continuous_const
                  · exact h₁₂
                  · intro x
                    by_cases h : x = 0
                    · simp_all
                    · simp_all [pow_two]
                      <;> norm_num
                      <;> nlinarith
                exact h₁₃
              exact h₁₁
          rw [h₁₀]
          -- Compute the integral of 1/x
          have h₁₁ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
            -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus
            have h₁₂ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
              -- Compute the antiderivative and evaluate at the bounds
              have h₁₃ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
                rw [← Real.log_div (by positivity) (by positivity)]
                <;> simp [intervalIntegral.integral_comp_div_left (fun x => 1 : ℝ → ℝ)]
                <;> field_simp [Real.log_div, Real.log_one, sub_zero]
                <;> ring_nf

            have h₁₃ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = (1 / a - 1 / b : ℝ) := by
              -- Compute the antiderivative and evaluate at the bounds
              have h₁₄ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = ∫ x in a..b, (x : ℝ) ^ (-2 : ℝ) := by

                <;> simp_all [Real.rpow_neg, Real.rpow_two]
                <;> field_simp [Real.rpow_neg, Real.rpow_two]

simp only [Set.mem_Icc]
    intro h
    have h₂ : min a b ≤ (0 : ℝ) := by
      cases' h with h₃ h₄
      linarith
    linarith
  
  have h₁ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = log b + 1 / a - log a - 1 / b := by
    have h₂ : a ≠ 0 := by linarith
    have h₃ : b ≠ 0 := by linarith
    have h₄ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) := rfl
    rw [h₄]
    -- Compute the integral of 1/x + 1/x² from a to b
    have h₅ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = (log b - log a) + (1 / b - 1 / a) := by
      -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus to evaluate the integral
      have h₅₁ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = (∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ)) + ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) := by
        -- Split the integral into two parts
        apply intervalIntegral.integral_add
        · -- Prove that 1/x is continuous on [a, b]
          apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
          continuity
        · -- Prove that 1/x² is continuous on [a, b]
          apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
          continuity
      rw [h₅₁]
      -- Evaluate the integral of 1/x from a to b
      have h₅₂ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
        have h₅₂₁ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
          -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus
          have h₅₂₂ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
            rw [integral_one_div] <;>
            (try norm_num) <;>
            (try linarith) <;>
            (try simp_all) <;>
            (try field_simp) <;>
            (try ring_nf)
          exact h₅₂₂
        exact h₅₂₁
      -- Evaluate the integral of 1/x² from a to b
      have h₅₃ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = 1 / b - 1 / a := by
        have h₅₃₁ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = 1 / b - 1 / a := by
          -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus
          have h₅₃₂ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = 1 / b - 1 / a := by
            have h₅₃₃ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = ∫ x in a..b, (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ 2 := by
              congr
              <;> ext <;> field_simp <;> ring
            rw [h₅₃₃]
            -- Use the power rule for integration
            have h₅₃₄ : ∫ x in a..b, (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ 2 = 1 / b - 1 / a := by
              -- Compute the integral using the fundamental theorem of calculus
              have h₅₃₅ : ∫ x in a..b, (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ 2 = ( - (1 : ℝ) / b) - (- (1 : ℝ) / a) := by
                -- Use the antiderivative of x⁻¹²
                rw [intervalIntegral.integral_comp_inv (fun x => x ^ 2)]
                <;> simp [ha.ne', hb.ne']
                <;> field_simp [ha.ne', hb.ne']
                <;> ring_nf
                <;> norm_num
                <;> linarith
              rw [h₅₃₅]
              <;> ring_nf
              <;> field_simp [ha.ne', hb.ne']
              <;> ring_nf
              <;> norm_num
              <;> linarith
            rw [h₅₃₄]
          exact h₅₃₂
        exact h₅₃₁
      -- Combine the results
      rw [h₅₂, h₅₃]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> field_simp [h₂, h₃]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith
    -- Simplify the expression to match the target form
    have h₆ : (log b - log a) + (1 / b - 1 / a) = log b + 1 / a - log a - 1 / b := by
      ring_nf
      <;> field_simp [ha.ne', hb.ne']
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith
    rw [h₅]
    linarith
  
  rw [h₁]
  <;>
  simp_all
  <;>
  linarith

linarith
      have h₄ : 0 < a := ha
      linarith
    · -- Case: b ≤ a
      have h₃ : 0 ≥ b := by linarith
      have h₄ : 0 < b := hb
      linarith
  sorry

simp [uIcc_eq_empty, ha.le, hb.le, ha, hb]
    <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try linarith) <;>
    (try
      {
        intro h
        have h₁ : (a : ℝ) ≤ b := by linarith
        have h₂ : a > 0 := ha
        have h₃ : b > 0 := hb
        have h₄ : (0 : ℝ) < a := ha
        have h₅ : (0 : ℝ) < b := hb
        linarith
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        norm_num at *
        <;>
        (try linarith)
      })

simp only [uIcc, Icc, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h₁ ⊢
      <;>
      (try { cases' le_total a b with hab hab <;> simp_all [hab] <;>
        (try { tauto }) <;>
        (try { linarith }) }) <;>
      tauto
    cases h₂ with
    | inl h₂ =>
      have h₃ : 0 < a := ha
      linarith
    | inr h₂ =>
      have h₃ : 0 < b := hb
      have h₄ : a ≤ b := by
        cases' le_total a b with hab hab <;> simp_all [uIcc, Icc, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;>
        (try { contradiction }) <;>
        (try { linarith })
      have h₅ : a ≤ 0 := by
        simp only [uIcc, Icc, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h₁
        cases' le_total a b with hab hab <;> simp_all [hab]
        <;>
        (try { nlinarith }) <;>
        (try { linarith })
        <;>
        (try { nlinarith })
      linarith

linarith [h.1]
  have h₂ : 0 < a := ha
  linarith
